models.name: OC4 + Infrastructure (small) + Disruption
models.description: State-sponsored APT (OC4) targeting regional power utilities with the goal of causing operational disruption. This scenario models sophisticated actors with nation-state resources attempting to compromise SCADA systems and industrial control infrastructure.

models.name: OC3 + SME + Ransomware
models.description: Cyber syndicate (OC3) conducting ransomware attacks against small and medium enterprises. This scenario models organized criminal groups deploying ransomware-as-a-service against targets with limited security resources.

models.name: OC2 + Financial + Data Breach
models.description: Small group threat actors (OC2) targeting financial institutions for data exfiltration. Models opportunistic attackers seeking customer PII and financial records.

models.name: OC4 + Healthcare + IP Theft
models.description: State-sponsored actors targeting healthcare research facilities for intellectual property theft, including pharmaceutical research and vaccine development data.

models.name: OC1 + Retail + Payment Fraud
models.description: Individual threat actors conducting payment card fraud against retail point-of-sale systems. Models lone hackers or small groups seeking financial gain through card skimming.

models.name: OC3 + Manufacturing + Supply Chain
models.description: Cyber syndicates compromising manufacturing supply chains to introduce malicious components or disrupt production processes.

models.name: OC4 + Government + Espionage
models.description: Nation-state actors conducting cyber espionage against government agencies, seeking classified information and intelligence assets.

models.name: OC2 + Education + DDoS
models.description: Small groups conducting distributed denial-of-service attacks against educational institutions, often for ideological reasons or extortion.

models.name: OC3 + Telecom + Infrastructure Attack
models.description: Organized criminal groups targeting telecommunications infrastructure for large-scale fraud, interception, or service disruption.
